meta.title: Legitimacy: a comparative dashboard
meta.description: Comparative dashboard of V. Zelensky's and V. Putin's legitimacy across four axes: legal-electoral, international, domestic support, territorial control and rule of law.
meta.og_alt: Legitimacy radar over 4 axes: Zelensky 76 vs Putin 40 (as of 2026-06-06).

hero.lede: Four legitimacy axes, symmetric criteria, links to primary sources. No editorial opinions — only quotes from documents, polls, and international indices.
hero.placeholder: ⏳ The chart will be available on Day 3, once we populate data/axes.yaml with scores.
hero.note: Axes A and B — calculated from sources (high confidence). C and D — preliminary pending polls and indices (Days 4-5).
hero.history_note: Total score = weighted mean of 4 axes (0.25 each). Quarterly snapshots from the 2022 invasion to now. Vertical markers mark key events.

axis_a.title: Legal-electoral legitimacy
axis_a.intro: Constitutional basis of election, mandate, conduct of elections, term length. What we count: turnout, vote share, opposition admittance, OSCE/ODIHR assessments, legal grounds for extending the term.

axis_b.title: International recognition
axis_b.intro: Recognition as head of state, international court orders, sanctions regimes, membership in international fora. Sources: ICC, UN, official EU/G7 decisions.

axis_c.title: Domestic support (trust)
axis_c.intro: Time series of trust ratings from independent pollsters. UA: KIIS, Razumkov, Rating. RU: Levada (foreign agent) separated from VTsIOM/FOM (state-owned). With confidence intervals.
axis_c.note: UA: KIIS, Razumkov, Rating (all independent). RU: Levada (independent, 'foreign agent') + VTsIOM / FOM (state-owned). Each point is a real poll report; linear interpolation between points.

axis_d.title: Territorial control and rule of law
axis_d.intro: V-Dem Liberal Democracy Index, Freedom House, RSF Press Freedom Index, % of controlled territory. Year-by-year dynamics.
axis_d.deepstate_note: External source — deepstatemap.live (Ukrainian crowdsourced project). Updated several times a day.

methodology.title: Methodology

p1.title: Symmetry.
p1.body: Every criterion applies to both actors under the same conditions.

p2.title: Attribution.
p2.body: Every score is click-through to the formula and to the primary source.

p3.title: Separating pollsters.
p3.body: Independent and state-owned polls are shown separately with labels.

p4.title: Factual tone.
p4.body: Epithets only as quoted text with quotation marks and a link.

methodology.contact: Found an error in the data or sources? Drop a note — we fix it and add it to the changelog.
